key_insight: EquiFiLM adds a simple charge-conditioning FiLM block to equivariant force fields so a pretrained foundation MLFF can learn driven, non-equilibrium physics without breaking E(3)-equivariance.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, DFT-accurate simulations of charge-dependent materials processes such as photoexcitation, charge injection, and field-driven dynamics with only a small amount of new labeled data.
method: Insert per-layer FiLM modulation on scalar channels of an equivariant backbone.
Condition the force field continuously on external variables such as charge.
Apply the recipe to MACE-MatPES to create E-MACE for charged liquid water.
evidence_signal: On four training charges, E-MACE reduced force RMSE from 21.3 to 6.96 meV/Å and energy RMSE from 6.1 to 0.1 meV/atom versus a baseline, while maintaining stable dynamics and strong interpolation/extrapolation performance on seven held-out charges.
watch_item: Performance depends on having scalar channels in the backbone and on the quality/coverage of the limited charge-conditioned training set, especially when extrapolating beyond the trained charge range.
paper_title: EquiFiLM: Charge-Conditioned Equivariant Force Fields via Feature-wise Linear Modulation
